title: Shepherd of Hermas
subtitle: The Pastor
attribution: Hermas, brother of Pope Pius I
significance: One of the most popular early Christian writings; considered canonical by some early church fathers including Irenaeus and Origen
key_themes: The Church as an aged woman becoming young
The tower as symbol of the Church
Two ways: narrow way of life, broad way of destruction
The Shepherd as the Son of God
theological_importance: Discusses possibility of repentance after baptism
Shows early church structure and practices
Important for understanding early Christian piety
notable_features: Very popular in early church - most widely read non-canonical text
Codex Sinaiticus includes it after New Testament
Codex Claromontanus lists it as part of New Testament

sections.title: Mandate 2 - No Evil-speaking
sections.preview: Second Mandate: Keep away from evil-speaking, for it is the mother of all evils. For if anyone is evil-spoken of, immediately the demon of anger is aroused against him. Keep this commandment, therefor...

sections.title: Mandate 3 - Truth
sections.preview: Third Mandate: Love truth and let no lie dwell in your heart, for the lie is from the devil, and the truth is from the Lord. Speak the truth to one another, and you will be established in your faith.

sections.title: Mandate 6 - Faith
sections.preview: Sixth Mandate: Keep this commandment concerning faith: First of all, believe that God is one, who created and established all things, and made out of what did not exist everything that is. Believe the...

sections.title: Mandate 7 - Fear God
sections.preview: Seventh Mandate: Fear the Lord and keep his commandments. By fearing the Lord you will live, and by keeping his commandments you will be justified. Do not fear the devil, for he cannot harm those who ...

sections.title: Mandate 8 - No Double-mindedness
sections.preview: Eighth Mandate: I told you that the creatures of God are double, for restraint is necessary in the case of all things. You must restrain yourself from evil, and not be double-minded or doubtful. For d...

sections.title: Mandate 9 - Joy
sections.preview: Ninth Mandate: Put away sorrow from yourself, for it is the sister of doubt and anger. Be joyful always, and pray with a cheerful heart, and God will hear you. For a sorrowful heart dries up the soul.

sections.title: Mandate 10 - Prophets
sections.preview: Tenth Mandate: Concerning the prophetic spirit, distinguish carefully: Every prophet who speaks in the spirit is examined by the spirit. If the prophet speaks what is right, he is of God. But if he as...

sections.title: Mandate 11 - Good Desires
sections.preview: Eleventh Mandate: Remove every evil desire from your heart, but put on the desire which is good and holy. For if you put on the good desire, you will hate the evil one, and will be able to conquer it.

sections.title: Mandate 12 - Righteousness
sections.preview: Twelfth Mandate: Put on desire for righteousness, and be armed with the fear of the Lord. For the fear of the Lord drives away sin, and where sin is absent, there righteousness dwells.
